thesis: Deep-value large-cap (very high cheapness z) where analyst revisions are strongly rising and crude/gas economics are stabilising — a low-multiple re-rating candidate that anchors the energy sleeve.
falsification: Crude rolls over and the rising-revision trend reverses, or the value gap persists for 2+ quarters with no estimate upgrades coming through.

thesis: Diversified-metals leader with the best combination of price momentum, reasonable valuation and rising analyst revisions; core expression of the commodity up-cycle.
falsification: Base-metal prices roll over and momentum and revisions both turn down; or a balance-sheet/dividend stress event re-rates it lower.

thesis: Aluminium/Novelis franchise that is cheap on cross-sectional value with rising revisions and decent momentum — a quality way to own the metals up-cycle.
falsification: Aluminium spreads compress, Novelis guidance disappoints, and momentum/revisions both fade.

thesis: Low-cost iron-ore miner with strongly rising revisions and supportive valuation; geared to domestic steel-capex demand.
falsification: Iron-ore prices and the revision trend turn down together, or captive-mining/pricing policy shocks the earnings path.

thesis: Integrated low-cost aluminium/alumina play with rising analyst revisions and positive momentum — a second metals leg diversifying VEDL/HINDALCO.
falsification: Alumina/aluminium prices fall and the revision momentum rolls over.

thesis: Cash-generative energy monopoly, cheap with rising analyst revisions and a high payout — adds yield and lowers volatility inside the commodity tilt.
falsification: Volume/price disappointment or an accelerated energy-transition narrative drives revisions and momentum negative.

thesis: Deep-value PSU steelmaker with above-peer momentum and supportive revisions — high-beta leg of the materials bet, sized smaller for that reason.
falsification: Steel prices fall, margins compress, and momentum/revisions both turn down.

thesis: Power-equipment/electrification leader riding the T&D and data-centre capex cycle with rising analyst revisions and strong momentum — core structural holding.
falsification: Order inflows slow, margins disappoint, and revisions/momentum fade; or the multiple de-rates from an already-rich level.

thesis: Pure-play grid/HVDC equipment franchise with strong momentum and positive revisions — leverages the same power-capex theme as CGPOWER from a different sub-segment.
falsification: Capex orders slow or the very high valuation de-rates while momentum stalls.

thesis: Transmission-and-distribution equipment maker with strong momentum and rising revisions, geared to grid build-out; third diversified leg of the power-capex theme.
falsification: T&D ordering decelerates and momentum/revisions reverse, or rich valuation corrects sharply.

thesis: Market-leading cables/wires franchise with positive revisions and steady momentum — a consumer-of-capex play with a cleaner growth profile.
falsification: Copper/input-cost spikes squeeze margins or B2B demand slows and revisions turn down.

thesis: Largest power generator, cheap with a growing renewables build-out and modest positive revisions — a low-volatility anchor within the energy/power sleeve.
falsification: Regulated-return cuts or project delays push revisions negative and growth stalls.

thesis: Himadri specialty chemicals — niche leader with strongly rising analyst revisions and EV/battery-material optionality; a higher-growth satellite in materials.
falsification: Revision momentum stalls, battery-material optionality disappoints, or the small-name volatility breaks the momentum.

thesis: Listed exchange compounding on rising cash/derivatives volumes with strong momentum and rising revisions — a structural capital-markets winner diversifying the financials core.
falsification: Regulatory tightening on derivatives volumes or a market-volume slump reverses revenue growth and revisions.

thesis: Dominant commodity-derivatives exchange with strong momentum and rising revisions — operating-leverage story as volumes and new products scale.
falsification: Volume growth stalls, tech/regulatory disruption hits market share, and revisions turn down.

thesis: Highest-quality large private bank with steady ROE and stable revisions — the core diversified financial holding that lowers book beta versus the cyclical sleeve.
falsification: Credit costs rise sharply, NIMs compress, and asset-quality deterioration drives revisions down.

thesis: Improving-franchise private bank trading cheaper than peers with positive revisions — a value-tilted complement to ICICI in the financials core.
falsification: Integration/asset-quality stumbles push revisions negative or the valuation discount stays stuck.

thesis: Deep-value PSU banking giant with strong cheapness and a cleaned-up book — a value leg in financials; sized modestly because revisions are only neutral.
falsification: Asset quality re-deteriorates, treasury losses hit, or government-policy overhang re-rates it lower.

thesis: Conservatively-run premium private bank with steadying revisions — quality ballast in the financials sleeve even though momentum is unexciting.
falsification: Growth keeps lagging peers and revisions turn down, making the premium multiple indefensible.

thesis: High-growth diversified NBFC with strongly rising analyst revisions — a revision-led financials add where I expect price to follow estimates.
falsification: Credit costs rise in the NBFC cycle or growth slows and the rising-revision trend stops.

thesis: Cheap commercial-vehicle financier with a strong franchise but only neutral momentum and softening revisions — retained at a reduced weight for diversification.
falsification: CV cycle weakens, credit costs rise, and revisions deteriorate further.

thesis: Steady mid-cap-quality private bank with neutral valuation and revisions — held at a reduced weight purely for diversification, not conviction.
falsification: NIM compression or asset-quality slippage turns revisions negative.

thesis: Best-in-class consumer NBFC with durable growth; momentum is weak and revisions only neutral so it is held at a modest weight as a quality option on a re-rating.
falsification: Growth decelerates, credit costs normalise higher, and revisions keep falling.

thesis: Mid-cap IT leader with very strong rising revisions while price has sold off — a tactical bet that lagging momentum reverts toward improving estimates.
falsification: Deal momentum/guidance disappoints, revisions stop rising, and price keeps falling — then exit, do not average down.

thesis: Best-quality large-cap IT services name with rising revisions despite poor momentum — a defensive revision-led add that also lowers book beta.
falsification: IT discretionary demand stays weak, revisions roll over, and the price downtrend extends.

thesis: Margin-recovery story in IT with rising revisions — held at a reduced weight as the higher-risk leg of the revision-led tech sleeve.
falsification: Margin recovery stalls, large deals slip, and revisions turn negative.

thesis: High-quality IT franchise retained small for index-diversification; weak momentum and neutral revisions argue against a benchmark-or-above weight right now.
falsification: Demand recovers and revisions inflect up — in which case I would re-add, not the falsifier of the current small weight.

thesis: Premium two-wheeler/export franchise with rising revisions — a revision-supported auto holding levered to export and premiumisation recovery.
falsification: Export demand or domestic 2W volumes disappoint and revisions roll over.

thesis: Diversified global auto-component supplier with rising revisions and an acquisition-led growth path — a structural auto-supply-chain holding.
falsification: Global auto production weakens, integration disappoints, and revisions turn down.

thesis: Best-execution two-wheeler maker with an EV ramp and positive revisions — held at a modest weight as a quality auto exposure.
falsification: EV economics disappoint or volume growth slows and revisions fade.

thesis: SUV and tractor leader with a strong product cycle but currently weak momentum and only neutral revisions — retained small pending a momentum inflection.
falsification: SUV demand cools, tractor cycle stays weak, and revisions turn negative.

thesis: Asset-light e-commerce platform scaling toward profitability, screening very cheap; held meaningfully but trimmed because revisions are only neutral.
falsification: Take-rate/monetisation disappoints, losses widen, and revisions deteriorate.

thesis: High-quality fast-fashion retailer with a long runway, retained at a reduced weight because momentum has faded and valuation is full.
falsification: Same-store-sales growth slows sharply and revisions turn down at a still-rich multiple.

thesis: Dominant branded jewellery and lifestyle compounder with positive revisions — a quality consumer-discretionary anchor that steadies the book.
falsification: Gold-price volatility or competitive intensity hits jewellery margins and revisions fall.

thesis: India's pharma leader with a specialty pipeline — a low-beta defensive holding that diversifies the cyclical tilt; signals neutral so weight is modest.
falsification: Specialty pipeline or US-generic pricing disappoints and revisions turn down.

thesis: Leading hospital and pharmacy/digital-health franchise with structural demand growth — defensive growth ballast; neutral momentum so sized modestly.
falsification: Occupancy/ARPOB growth slows or the digital-health cash burn re-rates it lower with falling revisions.

thesis: Dominant paints franchise where analysts are upgrading strongly even as price has lagged on competitive-intensity fears — a small contrarian revision-led add.
falsification: New-entrant competition keeps compressing margins and the strong revisions reverse; price downtrend continues.

thesis: Staples leader held small as low-beta diversification; weak momentum and soft revisions argue against an overweight in a pro-cyclical book.
falsification: Rural/volume recovery stalls and revisions keep falling — though as a low-beta hedge the small weight is defensible even then.

thesis: Telecom duopoly beneficiary with ARPU-led growth and pricing power — a defensive structural holding kept small while momentum is soft.
falsification: Tariff/competition pressure or capex overhang turns revisions and momentum down together.

thesis: Diversified conglomerate (energy/retail/telecom) held near benchmark-light to manage tracking risk; weak momentum and neutral revisions argue against an overweight now.
falsification: Retail/Jio growth disappoints or O2C margins compress and revisions stay negative.

thesis: Dominant defence-aerospace franchise with a large order book, retained at a reduced weight because momentum and revisions have both cooled from earlier highs.
falsification: Order execution/inflow slows and revisions turn negative at a still-elevated multiple.

thesis: Large integrated steelmaker, cheap with improving Europe/India spreads — a smaller, higher-beta complement to SAIL/JINDAL exposure within the metals tilt.
falsification: Steel spreads compress, Europe drags, and momentum/revisions turn down.

thesis: Thermal-power IPP with strong price momentum but falling analyst revisions; retained only at a token weight because the revision signal contradicts the momentum.
falsification: Revisions keep falling and the momentum breaks — the falling-revision signal is already the warning; further deterioration triggers a full exit.
